title: papaver alpinum subvar. rubicundum
intro: Papaver alpinum subvar. rubicundum is a subspecies of the alpine poppy plant, characterized by its vibrant red and yellow flowers that bloom in the mountainous regions of Europe. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Papaver alpinum subvar. rubicundum include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and promote wound healing. The plant can be prepared as a tincture, infusion, or salve, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote respiratory health and soothe skin irritations.
